title: SENTINEL: AI Reasoning Observatory for MCP Agent Governance (v1.0.0)
description:
SENTINEL is an MCP-native governance layer for AI agents. It audits the reasoning behind a pending agent decision — scoring signal fidelity (was evidence complete and current?), matching known failure signatures, computing historical reliability, and assigning an authority tier (FULL_AUTO, ACT_AND_NOTIFY, HUMAN_REQUIRED, QUARANTINE) — before irreversible actions commit.

This repository is the MCP-native reference implementation of the mid-reasoning interception framework described in the companion paper, SENTINEL: A Mid-Reasoning Interception Framework for Auditing Medical AI Agents in Production. It is deployed behind agentgateway and exposes four MCP tools (sentinel_evaluate, sentinel_reliability, sentinel_patterns, sentinel_pull_decisions) usable by any MCP client.

Release v1.0.0 archives the first end-to-end working build: Go MCP server, agentgateway CEL-based authorization, dashboard UI, Datadog / Braintrust / Cleric / ElevenLabs integrations, and a reproducible start_demo.sh entry point.

creators.name: Espira, Andrew
creators.affiliation: Kustode; Department of Data Science, Saint Peter's University

notes: If you use SENTINEL, please cite both the software (this DOI) and the companion paper DOI listed under related identifiers.
